hero.title: Your path to recovery starts here
hero.subtitle: Specialized center for sports medicine, rehabilitation and physical therapy in Šabac

services.title: Our Services
services.subtitle: Complete sports medicine care in one place
services.label: Services

fizikalna-terapija.title: Physical Therapy
fizikalna-terapija.shortDescription: Modern physical therapy methods for faster recovery and pain relief.
fizikalna-terapija.description: Physical therapy uses physical agents such as heat, cold, ultrasound, electrotherapy and laser for treating injuries and chronic conditions. Our team uses state-of-the-art equipment for optimal results.
fizikalna-terapija.process: Initial assessment → Treatment plan → Sessions → Progress evaluation

faq.question: How many physical therapy sessions are needed?
faq.answer: The number of sessions depends on the diagnosis. Typically 10-15 sessions are needed for optimal results, but the plan is tailored to individual needs.

faq.question: Is physical therapy painful?
faq.answer: Most treatments are painless or minimally uncomfortable. Our therapists always adjust the intensity to your comfort level.

faq.question: When can I expect improvement?
faq.answer: Many patients notice improvement after 3-5 sessions, but full effects are achieved after completing the treatment cycle.

manuelna-terapija.title: Manual Therapy
manuelna-terapija.shortDescription: Hands-on techniques for joint, soft tissue and spinal mobilization.
manuelna-terapija.description: Manual therapy encompasses specialized hands-on techniques for mobilization and manipulation of joints, soft tissues and the spine. Effective in treating back, neck and joint pain.
manuelna-terapija.process: Functional assessment → Diagnosis → Manual treatment → Home exercises

faq.question: Is manual therapy safe?
faq.answer: Yes, manual therapy is completely safe when performed by a qualified therapist. We use proven techniques adapted to your condition.

faq.question: Who can benefit from manual therapy?
faq.answer: Manual therapy is effective for anyone with back, neck, or joint pain, or mobility issues.

faq.question: How long does the effect of one session last?
faq.answer: The effect of a single session can last several days to a week. A series of sessions provides longer-lasting results.

kineziterapija.title: Kinesitherapy
kineziterapija.shortDescription: Movement therapy — individualized exercise programs for recovery and prevention.
kineziterapija.description: Kinesitherapy is movement therapy using specific exercises tailored to your condition. Programs are individually designed for injury rehabilitation, strength improvement and re-injury prevention.
kineziterapija.process: Functional status assessment → Individual program → Guided exercises → Progression

faq.question: Can I do the exercises at home too?
faq.answer: Yes, part of the kinesitherapy program includes home exercises that your therapist will explain and demonstrate in detail.

faq.question: How often should I attend kinesitherapy?
faq.answer: We recommend 2-3 times per week for optimal results, but frequency is adjusted to your condition and progress.

faq.question: Is kinesitherapy suitable for older adults?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Programs are adapted to age and fitness level. Kinesitherapy is extremely beneficial for maintaining mobility in older adults.

dijagnostika.title: Diagnostics
dijagnostika.shortDescription: Detailed sports medicine diagnostics for precise condition assessment.
dijagnostika.description: Sports medicine diagnostics includes complete evaluation of your condition using modern diagnostic methods. Accurate diagnosis is the foundation for an effective treatment plan.
dijagnostika.process: Medical history → Clinical exam → Functional testing → Report and plan

faq.question: What does sports medicine diagnostics include?
faq.answer: Diagnostics includes detailed medical history, clinical examination, functional testing, and additional diagnostic methods as needed for precise assessment.

faq.question: Do I need a referral for diagnostics?
faq.answer: No, you can come directly without a referral. We recommend bringing any previous medical documentation you may have.

faq.question: How long does the diagnostic exam take?
faq.answer: A complete diagnostic assessment takes 60-90 minutes, depending on the complexity of the case and tests required.

sportska-rehabilitacija.title: Sports Rehabilitation
sportska-rehabilitacija.shortDescription: Complete sports injury rehabilitation — from injury to return to play.
sportska-rehabilitacija.description: Sports rehabilitation is a comprehensive recovery program from sports injuries. We combine physical therapy, kinesitherapy and functional training for safe return to sport.
sportska-rehabilitacija.process: Injury evaluation → Recovery phase → Functional training → Return-to-play protocol

faq.question: How long does sports injury rehabilitation take?
faq.answer: Duration depends on the type and severity of injury. Minor injuries require 4-6 weeks, while major injuries (e.g., ACL) may require 6-12 months for full recovery.

faq.question: When can I return to training?
faq.answer: Return to training is determined based on a return-to-play protocol. Our team monitors your progress and gives the green light only when you're physically ready.

faq.question: Do you cover all types of sports injuries?
faq.answer: Yes, we work with all types of sports injuries — from muscle strains and ligament injuries to post-operative rehabilitation.

masaza.title: Massage
masaza.shortDescription: Sports and therapeutic massage for recovery, relaxation and prevention.
masaza.description: We offer sports massage, deep tissue massage and therapeutic massage tailored to your needs. Ideal for post-training recovery, tension relief and injury prevention.
masaza.process: Consultation → Technique selection → Treatment → Maintenance advice

faq.question: What is the difference between sports and therapeutic massage?
faq.answer: Sports massage focuses on muscle groups active in sports using more intense techniques, while therapeutic massage treats specific pain conditions with gentler approaches.

faq.question: How often should I get a massage?
faq.answer: For athletes we recommend 1-2 times per week. For general maintenance, once every 2-4 weeks is sufficient.

faq.question: Is any preparation needed before a massage?
faq.answer: We recommend coming hydrated and not eating a large meal immediately before treatment. Comfortable clothing is preferred.

recovery-terapije.title: Recovery Therapies
recovery-terapije.shortDescription: Advanced recovery methods: cryotherapy, compression therapy, and more.
recovery-terapije.description: Recovery therapies use the most advanced methods to accelerate recovery. Including cryotherapy, compression therapy, infrared sauna and other advanced techniques.
recovery-terapije.process: Needs assessment → Modality selection → Treatment → Recovery protocol

faq.question: What is cryotherapy and is it safe?
faq.answer: Cryotherapy uses controlled cooling to reduce inflammation and accelerate recovery. It is completely safe under the supervision of our specialists.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple recovery methods?
faq.answer: Yes, we often combine methods for optimal results. For example, cryotherapy after training and compression therapy for muscle recovery.

faq.question: Do recovery therapies help recreational athletes too?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Recovery therapies are beneficial for anyone seeking faster recovery, whether you're a professional athlete or recreational exerciser.

testiranje-merenja.title: Testing & Measurements
testiranje-merenja.shortDescription: Functional testing and measurements for athletes — performance analysis and prevention.
testiranje-merenja.description: Complete functional testing and measurements for athletes and recreational exercisers. Analysis of strength, endurance, mobility and biomechanics for training optimization.
testiranje-merenja.process: Testing protocol → Measurements → Results analysis → Training recommendations

faq.question: What is tested during functional testing?
faq.answer: We test strength, endurance, mobility, balance, movement biomechanics, and sport-specific abilities relevant to your sport.

faq.question: How often should testing be repeated?
faq.answer: We recommend testing every 3-6 months to track progress, or before and after a training period/season.

faq.question: Can testing prevent injuries?
faq.answer: Yes, functional testing identifies muscle imbalances and weaknesses that can lead to injuries, enabling preventive work.

items.name: Nikola M.
items.result: Return to the field in 8 months
items.story: After ACL surgery, Nikola started rehabilitation at Sport Care Med. Through an individual kinesitherapy and sports rehabilitation program, he returned to training in 8 months — one month ahead of schedule.

items.name: Jelena S.
items.story: Jelena suffered from shoulder pain for months that prevented her from playing at a professional level. Through a combination of manual therapy, physical therapy, and targeted exercise program, the pain was completely eliminated in 6 weeks.

items.name: FK Šabac U19
items.story: FK Šabac’s youth team started working with Sport Care Med for injury prevention. Through regular functional testing and individual corrective programs, the team recorded 60% fewer injuries during the season.

timska-rehabilitacija.title: Team Rehabilitation
timska-rehabilitacija.shortDescription: Complete rehabilitation programs tailored to the needs of your sports team
timska-rehabilitacija.description: Our team rehabilitation program provides a comprehensive approach to athlete recovery. Each team member receives an individual rehabilitation plan, while our team coordinates with coaches and club medical staff. We monitor each player's progress and adjust protocols in real time, ensuring a faster and safer return to play.
includes: Individual rehabilitation plans for each player
timska-rehabilitacija.process: Initial team assessment → Individual plans → Daily rehabilitation → Readiness testing → Return to play
benefits: Faster return to play for athletes
Cost savings compared to individual treatments

stats.label: of players return to play faster

faq.question: How many players can be included in the program?
faq.answer: The program is scalable for teams of 15 to 50+ members. Pricing is adjusted based on team size.

faq.question: Do therapists come to the club or do players come to you?
faq.answer: We combine both approaches. Rehabilitation takes place at our center, but for acute cases and monitoring we can come to your facility.

faq.question: How does coordination with coaching staff work?
faq.answer: Regular weekly reports, joint meetings, and direct communication with coaches and fitness trainers.

fizioterapija-na-terenu.title: On-Site Physiotherapy
fizioterapija-na-terenu.shortDescription: Our therapists come to your training sessions and matches
fizioterapija-na-terenu.description: Our on-site physiotherapy service ensures a qualified therapist is present at all your training sessions and matches. Immediate injury response, professional taping and bandaging, and injury prevention during activities — all on-site. Your athletes receive instant medical support without having to visit the clinic.
fizioterapija-na-terenu.process: Schedule coordination → Therapist on location → Intervention as needed → Post-session report → Treatment recommendations

faq.question: How many training sessions per week do you cover?
faq.answer: We adapt to your schedule. Standard packages cover 3-5 training sessions per week plus matches.

faq.question: What equipment does the on-site therapist bring?
faq.answer: A complete therapeutic bag: tapes, bandages, ice, portable ultrasound, electrostimulation, and a first aid kit.

faq.question: Is the therapist present only during training?
faq.answer: The therapist arrives 30 minutes before training (for warm-up and prevention) and stays 15-30 minutes after to handle any injuries.

prevencija-povreda.title: Injury Prevention Programs
prevencija-povreda.shortDescription: Proactive approach to reducing injury risk in your team
prevencija-povreda.description: Prevention is the most effective and cost-efficient strategy in sports medicine. Our program includes complete screening of all team members at the start of the season, individual prevention programs, coach and player education, and regular monthly risk reports. We use proven protocols like FIFA 11+ for dramatic injury reduction.
includes: Screening of all players at season start
prevencija-povreda.process: Team screening → Risk analysis → Individual programs → Implementation → Monthly evaluation → Program correction

faq.question: How long does the initial screening take?
faq.answer: For a team of 25 players, the complete screening takes 1-2 days. It includes FMS, strength tests, flexibility, and previous injury assessment.

faq.question: Does the program include training for coaches?
faq.answer: Yes. We educate the coaching staff on risk signs, proper warm-up, and implementation of preventive exercises into daily training.

faq.question: How does prevention cost compare to treatment?
faq.answer: Our experience shows that every dollar invested in prevention saves 5 dollars in treatment and rehabilitation costs. Plus, players miss fewer training sessions and matches.

testiranje-performansi.title: Team Performance Testing
testiranje-performansi.shortDescription: Complete functional testing for all team members
testiranje-performansi.description: Our performance testing program provides a complete picture of each team member's physical abilities. We use FMS screening, strength and explosiveness measurements, cardio-respiratory testing, and movement analysis. Each athlete receives an individual report with recommendations, and the coaching team receives a summary overview for training planning.
testiranje-performansi.process: Schedule appointment → Team testing (1-2 days) → Data analysis → Individual reports → Coach presentation → Retesting after 3-6 months

faq.question: What tests are included in the battery?
faq.answer: FMS, vertical jump, 10m/30m sprint, Yo-Yo test, isokinetic tests, movement analysis, flexibility, and sport-specific tests.

faq.question: Can testing be done at our facility?
faq.answer: Most tests are portable and can be done in your gym or on the field. For specialized lab tests, we invite you to our center.

faq.question: How often should testing be repeated?
faq.answer: We recommend testing 2-3 times per year: pre-season, mid-season, and post-season. This gives a picture of progress and training effects.

tools.title: Interactive Tools
tools.subtitle: Free tools for recovery assessment and sport readiness

recoveryEstimator.title: Recovery Timeline Estimator
recoveryEstimator.shortDescription: Find out how long it takes to recover from your sports injury
recoveryEstimator.description: An interactive tool that estimates recovery time based on injury type, severity and your personal factors, and shows a phased rehabilitation plan.
recoveryEstimator.metaTitle: Sports Injury Recovery Timeline Estimator | Sport Care Med
recoveryEstimator.metaDescription: Free tool to estimate recovery time from sports injuries. Find out how long rehabilitation takes and what the recovery phases are.
recoveryEstimator.step1Subtitle: Choose the injury type closest to your condition
recoveryEstimator.previousInjury: Have you previously injured the same area?
recoveryEstimator.disclaimer: This estimate is for informational purposes only and does not replace a professional medical examination. For an accurate diagnosis and treatment plan, schedule an appointment with our specialists.

acute.title: Acute Phase / Protection
acute.description: Pain and swelling control, protecting injured tissue

earlyRehab.title: Early Rehabilitation
earlyRehab.description: Gradual return to mobility and gentle exercises

strengthening.title: Strengthening
strengthening.description: Progressive muscle strengthening and stability improvement

sportSpecific.title: Sport-Specific Training
sportSpecific.description: Sport-specific exercises and gradual intensity increase

returnToPlay.title: Return to Play
returnToPlay.description: Full return to training and competition

faq.question: How accurate is this recovery estimate?
faq.answer: This estimate is based on average recovery times from medical literature. Actual recovery time may vary depending on many factors. For an accurate assessment, we recommend a professional examination.

faq.question: What factors affect recovery speed?
faq.answer: Key factors include: injury severity, age, overall health, pre-injury fitness level, quality of rehabilitation, and adherence to the recovery plan.

faq.question: Can I speed up my recovery?
faq.answer: Yes, regular and professionally guided rehabilitation, proper nutrition, adequate rest, and gradual activity increase can significantly speed up recovery.

readinessChecklist.title: Return-to-Sport Readiness Checklist
readinessChecklist.shortDescription: Check if you're ready for a safe return to sports activities
readinessChecklist.description: An interactive checklist that evaluates your readiness for a safe return to sport based on pain, mobility, strength, function and psychological readiness.
readinessChecklist.metaTitle: Return-to-Sport Readiness Checklist | Sport Care Med
readinessChecklist.metaDescription: Free interactive checklist to evaluate whether you're ready for a safe return to sports activities after injury.
readinessChecklist.notReadyText: Your results indicate you need additional rehabilitation before returning to sport.
readinessChecklist.almostReadyText: You're on the right track, but some areas need more work before a full return.
readinessChecklist.readyText: Your results indicate you're ready for a safe return to sports activities!
readinessChecklist.bookSession: Book a session with our specialists
readinessChecklist.disclaimer: This checklist is for informational purposes only and does not replace a professional medical assessment. For final clearance to return to sport, consult with your doctor.

painSwelling.title: Pain & Swelling
painSwelling.description: Assessment of pain and swelling levels

items.fullRange: Full range of motion compared to uninjured side
items.fullSquat: Can perform full squat without restriction

strengthPower.title: Strength & Power
strengthPower.description: Assessment of muscle strength and explosiveness

items.singleLeg: Can perform single-leg exercises without difficulty
items.sprintFull: Can sprint at full speed without hesitation

items.fullIntensity: Can perform sport-specific drills at full intensity

psychologicalReadiness.title: Psychological Readiness
psychologicalReadiness.description: Assessment of mental state and readiness

items.noFear: No fear of re-injury during training

recommendations.painSwelling: Focus on pain and swelling management. Consider physical therapy and cryotherapy.
recommendations.rangeOfMotion: Work on improving mobility through stretching and mobilization exercises.
recommendations.strengthPower: Additional muscle strengthening is needed. Consult a physiotherapist for an exercise program.
recommendations.sportFunction: Gradually introduce sport-specific exercises. Work with a coach on movement technique.
recommendations.psychologicalReadiness: Consider speaking with a sports psychologist. Gradual exposure to sport situations can help.

faq.question: When is the right time to return to sport after injury?
faq.answer: The right time to return depends on many factors, including injury type, quality of rehabilitation and individual response to recovery. This checklist helps you objectively assess your readiness.

faq.question: What if I'm not ready according to the checklist?
faq.answer: If the checklist shows you're not fully ready, that's not a cause for concern. Focus on the weakest areas and continue rehabilitation. Returning to sport too early can lead to re-injury.

faq.question: Does this checklist replace a medical assessment?
faq.answer: No. This checklist is a self-assessment tool and does not replace a professional medical examination. We recommend sharing your results with your doctor or physiotherapist.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Sport Care Med respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act of Serbia and the GDPR.

dataCollected.title: Data we collect
dataCollected.body: We collect information you provide directly (name, email, phone number, address) when booking, submitting contact forms, subscribing to the newsletter or shopping. We also collect basic technical data about your device and visits to improve the site.

dataUse.title: How we use your data
dataUse.body: We use your data solely to deliver our services, process orders, respond to inquiries, send newsletters (with your consent) and meet legal obligations. We do not sell your data to third parties.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: The site uses essential cookies for the cart and session, plus analytics cookies to measure traffic. You may disable them in your browser settings.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: You have the right to access, correct, delete, restrict and port your data, as well as the right to object. Send requests to info@sportcaremed.rs.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For data protection inquiries contact info@sportcaremed.rs or write to the clinic address in Šabac.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using sportcaremed.rs you agree to the following terms. Please read them carefully.

use.title: Site use
use.body: Site content is for informational purposes and does not replace professional medical advice, diagnosis or treatment. Always consult a qualified physician for health decisions.

orders.title: Orders & payment
orders.body: Prices are in Serbian dinars (RSD) including VAT. Payment is available by card (RaiAccept) or cash on delivery. Orders are confirmed once a confirmation email is received.

returns.title: Returns & complaints
returns.body: You may return goods within 14 days of receipt, per the Consumer Protection Act. Items must be undamaged and in original packaging.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: Sport Care Med is not liable for damage arising from misuse of information, tools or products from the site. Users are responsible for their own decisions.
